facts.text: Manhasset sits atop the Harbor Hill Moraine, a terminal moraine formed by Wisconsin‑era glaciers that left the highest point in Nassau County at Harbor Hill.
The Long Island Railroad arrived in 1898, sparking a boom of wealthy New Yorkers who built country homes in Manhasset with easy access to Manhattan.
Manhasset’s name comes from a Native American word meaning “the island neighborhood,” reflecting its early settlers’ view of the area as a cluster of small islands.
The town’s historic Manhasset Valley School once served Gold Coast estate children and later became a center for the African‑American community before closing in the 1960s.
Manhasset’s high‑end shopping scene is famous for its concentration of distinct local merchants and premium boutiques, a hallmark of the hamlet’s upscale character.
Local theater lovers can catch productions by the Herricks Community Theater Group or the Landmark Theater in nearby Port Washington, adding a cultural touch to the area.
